summary.scope: Narrow pass-2: ratify-or-challenge trailing-wildcard semantic + attenuation algorithm + test-edit ratification + residual-findings disposition.

pass1_dispositions.g4-uc-1: accept — fix-agent's framing is defensible. The earlier draft DID have the zip-on-shorter bypass the auditor flagged, but the semantic claim that parent '*' (sole segment) grants unrestricted authority is an intentional Phase-1 choice consistent with UCAN's `top` ability. The real exploit window the pass-1 finding described — that the algorithm terminated after one segment regardless of child tail — is closed in the new algorithm; the 'single * permits everything' behavior is now a pinned-semantic, not an algorithmic accident. See new finding g4-p2-uc-1 for the narrow residual concern (documentation gap, not a code defect).
pass1_dispositions.g4-uc-2: partial — fix-agent correctly identified that the SAME underlying zip-on-shorter bug produced TWO different apparent bypasses: (a) the deterministic 'store:post' accepting 'store:post:write:delete' (now closed by the Greater/Less arms of the match) and (b) the semantic 'store:*' accepting 'store:anything:write:delete' (intentional trailing-wildcard suffix extension). The fix-agent preserves (b) as Phase-1 contract and closes (a) deterministically. Auditor pass-1 conflated these; I accept the disaggregation. The regression test `g4_uc_2_deterministic_regression_no_wildcard_parent_denies_extra_tail` is correctly scoped.
pass1_dispositions.g4-uc-3: open (deferred) — GrantScope::parse still accepts `store::write`, `:::`, `a::b`. Not re-raised as blocking because Phase-3 UCAN lands a proper lattice parser. Noted in new finding g4-p2-uc-4.
pass1_dispositions.g4-uc-4: accept — check_read default-method with Ok(()) added to the trait. ReadContext shape lands with synthetic_for_test. Rustdoc link resolves. NoAuth keeps default (permit). Phase-3 expansion point preserved. Finding closed.
pass1_dispositions.g4-uc-5: accept (deferred to G3) — the WriteContext still lacks a per-write `ops: Vec<WriteOp>` field, but the policy.rs rustdoc now EXPLICITLY calls out the G3 dependency with a `TODO(G3)` block naming the shape, the agent that owns it, and the consequence for compromise #1. Pre-landing a field without the wiring would freeze a shape G3 hasn't designed. Disposition 'defer' is defensible; re-raise at G3 pass-1.
pass1_dispositions.g4-uc-6: accept — DEFAULT_BATCH_BOUNDARY promoted from informational const to trait method `iterate_batch_boundary()` with default. Backends can tighten the bound. `tests/toctou_iteration.rs` now imports the pub const from benten-caps so the two sides stay in lockstep. The wall-clock-bound suggestion (min(100 iter, 5 min)) remains unaddressed; see new finding g4-p2-uc-2.
pass1_dispositions.g4-uc-7: accept — `tests/proptest_attenuation.rs` lands with 4 properties × 1024 cases + 1 deterministic regression. Properties cover: trailing-wildcard suffix extension, fabricated-tail denial, positive-chain (A→C under wildcard A), and the single-`*`-is-narrowing pin. Pass-rate verified locally (all 5 tests green). Residual concern on the transitivity property's strength is in new finding g4-p2-uc-3; it is not a blocker because it does not re-open the g4-uc-1/2 bypass class.
pass1_dispositions.g4-uc-8: accept — principal-aliasing vector closed. `issuer` is now a required public field on the struct-literal path; `new()` returns Self; both paths produce identical-CID grants. `grant_issuer_difference_changes_cid` in grant_uniqueness_on_cid.rs still exercises the sharp edge. The test-contract asymmetry flagged in pass-1 is retired.
pass1_dispositions.g4-uc-9: accept — `Denied` + `DeniedDetail` consolidated into a single struct variant `Denied { required, entity }` with both fields allowing empty strings for fixtures that don't care about payload. Audit pipelines can't accidentally match only one shape.
pass1_dispositions.g4-uc-10: open (deferred) — STUB_MARKER still has no release-build guardrail. Mirrored `TODO(phase-1-cleanup, G8)` anchors at both sides (benten-caps/src/lib.rs:82 and benten-engine/src/lib.rs:30). Acceptable for Phase 1; re-raise at G8.
pass1_dispositions.g4-uc-11: open (deferred to G3/G6) — no HLC reserve on WriteContext. Phase-3 scope.
pass1_dispositions.g4-uc-12: open (deferred to Phase 3) — no revocation-check trait hook. The `RevokedMidEval` error variant is plumbed for the G6 batch-boundary check; the sync-revocation surface (`Revoked`) is Phase 3.

reference_points_consulted: UCAN 0.10 ability-hierarchy spec (trailing `*` = 'any ability below this point' — consistent with fix-agent framing)
UCAN 1.0 draft (rs-ucan / Fission reference) where abilities are URI-path-like and `crud/*` subsumes `crud/read` etc.
ENGINE-SPEC §9 — attenuation 'narrow, never widen' + example uses slash-separated `post/*` (not colon-separated). Phase-1 uses `:` as a stand-in; Phase-3 lattice revisits.

.addl/phase-1/r1-triage.md — SC4 `requires`-property semantics + 'attack vector C3 delegation-chain widening'. No explicit wildcard-semantic mandate; Phase-1 implementer was free to choose within 'only narrow'.
ucan_semantics_ratification.verdict: Trailing-wildcard semantic IS consistent with UCAN in spirit. The divergence is that UCAN always qualifies `*` with a resource (you can't have `*` as a whole grant), whereas Benten Phase 1's flat colon-split tokenization permits a lone `*` scope. That's a broader unrestricted grant than UCAN permits at its top, but it's bounded by: (i) Phase 1 defaults to NoAuthBackend so no grants exist unless the operator wires a policy, (ii) Phase 3 replaces the whole check with a UCAN lattice, (iii) the proptest pins the semantic as intentional so a future regression is detected. Ratify.
ucan_semantics_ratification.phase_3_migration_risk: When Phase 3 UCAN lands, existing Phase-1 grants with scope `'*'` will need to be migrated to a UCAN root-ability scheme OR rejected at the lattice parser. The fix-agent's current implementation leaves that decision to Phase 3. Acceptable.

algorithm_correctness_audit.transitivity: Holds on positive chains WHERE all legs are positive. Hand-traced: A=`a:b:*`, B=`a:b:c`, C=`a:b:c:d`: A→B ok (trailing-wildcard), B→C denied (child extends concrete parent), A→C ok (trailing-wildcard). So transitivity A→B, B→C ⟹ A→C is vacuously true when any leg fails. The proptest's `prop_transitivity_on_positive_chain` asserts A→B and A→C directly — it does NOT chain through a B→C intermediate. That's technically a weaker property than full transitivity; see g4-p2-uc-3.
algorithm_correctness_audit.antisymmetry: Holds. If both A→B and B→A succeed, the only case where neither side has a trailing wildcard requires segment-wise equality (len+content). Mixed-wildcard cases (A trailing-*, B concrete) fail antisymmetry preconditions: B→A requires B's concrete segments to match A's `*` position, which fails the `p_seg != '*' && p_seg != c_seg` check on B's side. Hand-verified for 6 mixed cases.

edge_cases_audited.empty_parent_scope: GrantScope::parse('') rejected at construction. check_attenuation never sees empty. Safe.
edge_cases_audited.parent_is_lone_star: Intentional unrestricted grant (Phase-1 pinned semantic; Phase-3 lattice revisits).
edge_cases_audited.child_has_middle_star: Correctly denied. Parent `store:post:write` vs child `store:*:write`: i=1 p='post' != c='*' → denied. Child cannot use `*` to widen.
edge_cases_audited.mid_scope_wildcard_in_parent: Correctly permits. Parent `store:*:write` vs child `store:post:write` passes; parent `store:*:write` vs child `store:post:write:extra` correctly denied (Greater arm with no trailing wildcard).
edge_cases_audited.unicode_homograph: Not a bypass — byte-equality comparison denies `store:café` (NFC) vs `store:cafe\u{0301}` (NFD). Denial is the safe direction. Usability footgun, not a security one.
edge_cases_audited.parent_star_star: `*:*` permits children with ≥2 segments via the trailing-wildcard at i=1. Children with 1 segment denied (Less arm). Reasonable narrowness.

proptest_coverage_soundness.strengths: Property 1 (trailing-wildcard suffix extension) and property 2 (fabricated-tail denial) are non-tautological and directly exercise the g4-uc-2 bypass class. Property 4 (single-* pinned semantic) is a deterministic guard — correctly flagged as such in-source. Regression test g4_uc_2_deterministic_regression is tight.
proptest_coverage_soundness.weaknesses: Property 3 ('transitivity on positive chains') does not actually compose 3 scopes through a middleman — it generates A=prefix:* and independently asserts A→B and A→C, never testing that B→C is consistent with A→C. True 3-step transitivity would need: generate A, B, C; if A→B and B→C both succeed, assert A→C. Current shape is weaker but not unsound. See g4-p2-uc-3.
proptest_coverage_soundness.counterexample_classes_not_covered: Random-chain generation with length 2..8 (the pass-1 g4-uc-7 suggestion) was not adopted — only 3-node chains with one middleman. For Phase 1, the 4-property suite is sufficient because the algorithm's correctness properties are structural (prefix-equality + trailing-wildcard), not compositional. Phase 3 UCAN lattice will need the longer-chain proptest.

test_edits_ratification.capability_grant.rs: SURGICAL. Added `issuer` field to struct literals. Deterministic-CID assertion still asserts that byte-identical content (including same issuer) produces same CID. Nothing weakened.
test_edits_ratification.check_write_called_at_commit.rs: SURGICAL. CapError::DeniedDetail → CapError::Denied { required, entity } — shape preserved, payload preserved. Called-once-per-commit contract unchanged. Atomic-denial contract unchanged.
test_edits_ratification.error_code_mapping.rs: SURGICAL. `Denied { required, entity }` payload fields populated with empty strings for test; `NotImplemented { backend, lands_in_phase }` populated with UCANBackend + Phase 3. Code-mapping assertions structurally identical.
test_edits_ratification.noauth.rs: SURGICAL. `target_label` dropped (redundant with `label`). `pseudo_actor_label()` returns 'noauth' still asserted. Object-safety smoke still asserted.
test_edits_ratification.noauth_proptest.rs: SURGICAL minus one DELETION: the `prop_noauth_zero_alloc` test was removed because the helper returned constant 0, making the assertion tautological. This is a LEGITIMATE removal per TDD hygiene (a false-positive green test is worse than no test). Ratified on the condition that Phase 2 lands the tracking allocator behind `testing-alloc` feature flag and re-adds the proptest. Flag as a tracking obligation, not a weakening.
test_edits_ratification.policy_trait.rs: SURGICAL. Trait-object-safety on NoAuthBackend + UcanBackend still asserted; two-backend-same-trait still asserted. NotImplemented match still catches the new struct variant via `matches!(.., Err(CapError::NotImplemented { .. }))`.
test_edits_ratification.toctou_iteration.rs: SURGICAL. `DEFAULT_BATCH_BOUNDARY` now imports from `benten_caps::DEFAULT_BATCH_BOUNDARY` (pass-1 g4-cr-3 requested this lockstep). 100-iteration boundary regression still fires at the batch-2 boundary via write 101. Nothing weakened; in fact strengthened (cross-crate const binding).
test_edits_ratification.ucan_stub_messages.rs: SURGICAL. Four tests: errors-cleanly, message-names-phase-and-alternative, distinct-code, ON_ERROR-routing. All preserved with the new `NotImplemented { backend, lands_in_phase }` payload. Message assertion checks substrings 'Phase 3' and 'NoAuthBackend' — these are now produced by thiserror formatting against the struct fields, not a hardcoded string. Ratified.
test_edits_ratification.call_attenuation.rs: SURGICAL. Three tests: chained-call-exceeding-denies, chained-call-within-permits, noauth-does-not-fire. Still calls `benten_caps::testing::check_attenuation` via the back-compat re-export. Scope strings unchanged (`store:post:read`, `store:post:write`, `store:post:*`). Attack-scenario coverage preserved. The positive-boundary case (parent `store:post:*`, child `store:post:read`) does NOT by itself exercise the g4-uc-2 bypass; that's why proptest_attenuation.rs was added. Ratified.

findings.claim: The rustdoc for the trailing-wildcard branch does not mark the single-`*`-parent case as a Phase-1-specific unrestricted-authority semantic that Phase 3 UCAN lattice will constrain.
findings.detail: A new reader of attenuation.rs sees the trailing-wildcard permits 'the child's remaining tail (including zero tail segments)'. Applied to parent=['*'] (a 1-segment parent where the sole segment IS the trailing wildcard), this means parent `'*'` permits ANY child. The behavior is pinned by `prop_parent_single_wildcard_is_still_narrowing`, but the attenuation.rs module docs do not flag this as a 'Phase-1 root grant' case. Phase 3 will likely reject a lone `*` scope at parse time (UCAN does not permit ungrounded root grants). Without a comment flagging this, a future reviewer doing a cursory read could miss that `GrantScope::parse('*')` produces an unrestricted grant.
findings.suggested_fix: Add 4-6 lines to the attenuation.rs module docs under a heading like 'Phase-1 semantic decisions': 'Parent `'*'` (sole segment) is treated as a trailing wildcard and permits any child. This is an intentional Phase-1 unrestricted-authority marker consistent with UCAN's top ability; Phase 3 UCAN-lattice replacement will reject lone `'*'` scopes at parse time in favor of a grounded resource + ability pair.' Also consider a parallel note on `GrantScope::parse` pointing at this semantic.

findings.claim: `iterate_batch_boundary()` provides only an iteration-count bound, not a wall-clock bound. Named compromise #1's prose implies 'up to 100 iterations per snapshot' is fine for 100µs-per-iter workloads; a TRANSFORM-heavy handler at 1 iter/sec gives a 100-second TOCTOU window which exceeds financial-write-TTL-≤5-minutes only at large scale, but any 1 iter/10sec handler (plausible with an external CALL) pushes past 10 minutes.
findings.detail: The auditor brief mandates `Capability TTLs for financial/write operations must be ≤5 minutes`. The trait method's contract is purely iteration-count; there is no runtime check that 'N iterations AND ≤300 seconds' are the refresh cadence. Phase 3 UCAN will re-decide this; for Phase 1 this is a minor issue because (a) NoAuthBackend is the default and doesn't care, (b) UcanBackend is a stub. But once a real capability backend lands, the bound should be `min(iteration_count, wall_clock_seconds)`.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) extend the trait with `fn iterate_batch_wallclock_secs(&self) -> u64 { 300 }` so a policy can bound both axes, or (b) document on `iterate_batch_boundary`'s rustdoc that the first real capability backend MUST additionally enforce a wall-clock bound (because iteration-count alone is insufficient for slow handlers). A documented obligation is acceptable for Phase 1; a trait method is cleaner.

findings.claim: The transitivity property does not chain through an intermediate scope. It generates A=prefix:* and asserts A→B and A→C independently, never constructing a 3-step chain A→B→C where B's attenuation to C is non-trivial.
findings.detail: Pass-1 g4-uc-7 specifically asked for 'random chains of length 2..8 where each scope claims to narrow the last, asserting the effective scope is a structural subset of EVERY ancestor'. The current property generates two sibling concrete scopes (B=prefix:b_tail, C=prefix:c_tail) under a shared wildcard parent and asserts A permits both — which is just property 1 applied twice, not transitivity. A genuine transitivity proptest would: (1) generate a random parent A; (2) generate a random child B such that A→B succeeds; (3) generate a random grandchild C such that B→C succeeds; (4) assert A→C succeeds. Today the suite would miss a transitivity regression where (e.g.) mid-scope wildcards in different positions of A vs B chain incorrectly.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend prop_transitivity_on_positive_chain to a genuine 3-step chain: generate A (random scope 1-4 segments, possibly with trailing `*`); generate B by narrowing A one step (append one segment if A ends in `*`, OR reuse A exactly); generate C by narrowing B. Filter to the case where A→B and B→C both succeed; assert A→C succeeds. Accept a modest filter-rejection rate. Optionally add chain length 4..=6 at a lower case-count (256) for deeper coverage. Keep the current property as 'prop_one_step_wildcard_permits_siblings' since it IS a useful independent assertion.

findings.claim: GrantScope::parse still accepts strings with empty inner segments (`store::write`, `:::`, `:store`, `store:`). The attenuation algorithm then compares those segment-by-segment with empty strings, producing surprising results (e.g. parent `store::write` permits child `store::write` because split(':') produces ['store', '', 'write'] on both sides and they compare equal).
findings.detail: This is the pass-1 g4-uc-3 finding, which the fix-pass did not address. Not a bypass per se — attenuation still works segment-by-segment including on empty segments — but it is an encoding-trick surface: an audit pipeline that expects 'store:post:write' might see an attacker-submitted 'store:::post:write' that produces a DIFFERENT CID (different bytes) but parses successfully and attenuates identically to a hand-written scope the attacker knows the victim already trusts. Phase-3 UCAN will presumably reject this at the lattice parser. For Phase 1, a tightening of the parser would remove the encoding-trick axis entirely.
findings.suggested_fix: In GrantScope::parse, after trimming and non-empty check, reject any scope where `split(':').any(|seg| seg.is_empty())`. Add two unit tests: parse('store::write').is_err() and parse(':store').is_err() and parse('store:').is_err(). This is a 3-line change and closes a documented pass-1 concern.

residual_open_items_handoff.g4-uc-5_ops_field: Defer to G3. G3 mini-review MUST re-raise: does WriteContext.ops land? If G3 lands a transaction primitive that passes only a summary to check_write, the pre-write hook's stated purpose is undermined.
residual_open_items_handoff.g4-uc-10_stub_marker: Defer to G8. Mirrored TODOs at both sides anchor the cleanup.
residual_open_items_handoff.g4-uc-11_hlc_reserve: Defer to G3/G6 (HLC lands with the change-notification plumbing).
residual_open_items_handoff.g4-uc-12_revocation_hook: Phase 3 (benten-id). RevokedMidEval plumbed; Revoked is sync-layer code.
residual_open_items_handoff.g4-p2-uc-2_wallclock_bound: R4b or G6 pass-1: confirm the evaluator's ITERATE batch refresh honors BOTH iteration count AND a wall-clock ceiling.

notes: Narrow pass-2 scope honored. All pass-1 criticals are retired by the fix-pass (the underlying zip-on-shorter bug is genuinely closed). The trailing-wildcard semantic for lone `*` is ratified as intentional Phase-1 contract and pinned by proptest; the Phase-3 UCAN-lattice migration is a known inheritance. Four new minor findings (g4-p2-uc-1..4) are all documentation / tightening concerns — none re-open the wildcard-bypass class. Test edits are surgical; one DELETION (tautological alloc-count proptest) is a legitimate TDD-hygiene call. Advance G4 and continue G3/G5 dispatch. The g4-uc-5 ops-field surface is the single most important residual — re-raise at G3 pass-1.
